home.heroTitle: How Much Does Your Country Spend on Defense?
home.heroSubtitle: Explore and compare military budgets across 40+ nations. See where taxpayer money goes, who spends the most, and how your country stacks up.
home.trustedBy: Trusted by researchers, journalists, and policy analysts
home.understandingP1: Global defense spending surpassed $2 trillion annually, reflecting the growing complexity of modern security challenges. From conventional military forces to cyber defense and space operations, nations allocate significant portions of their national budgets to safeguard sovereignty and project power. Understanding these expenditures is essential for policymakers, researchers, journalists, and engaged citizens who want to follow how public funds are used.
home.understandingP2: Defense budgets vary dramatically by region and economic capacity. The United States alone accounts for roughly 40% of worldwide military spending, while NATO allies collectively aim to meet the alliance's 2% of GDP guideline. Meanwhile, countries in the Indo-Pacific, the Middle East, and Eastern Europe are increasing outlays in response to shifting geopolitical dynamics, territorial disputes, and emerging threats.
home.understandingP3: DefenseBudget Dash aggregates data from trusted sources including SIPRI and the World Bank so you can compare absolute spending figures, GDP ratios, and active military personnel across more than 40 countries in one place. Whether you are writing a research paper, preparing a policy brief, or simply curious about how your country stacks up, our interactive tools make it easy to explore the numbers.
home.transparencyP1: Transparent defense budgets strengthen democratic accountability. When citizens can see how much their government spends on the military and compare that figure to health, education, and infrastructure investments, they can make more informed decisions at the ballot box. Open data also reduces the risk of corruption and waste within defense procurement pipelines.
home.transparencyP2: For international relations, transparency serves as a confidence-building measure. Countries that publish detailed defense expenditures signal predictability to allies and adversaries alike, lowering the likelihood of miscalculation. Organizations like SIPRI, NATO, and the United Nations encourage member states to report military spending openly as part of broader arms-control and peacebuilding efforts.
home.transparencyP3: Tools like DefenseBudget Dash democratize access to this critical information. Instead of sifting through dense government reports and spreadsheets, users can visualize trends, rank countries by multiple metrics, and share their findings with colleagues or on social media in seconds. Our goal is to make defense spending data accessible, understandable, and actionable for everyone.

about.title: About DefenseBudget Dash
about.missionP1: DefenseBudget Dash is a free, open-data tool designed to make global defense spending information accessible and easy to understand for everyone — from researchers and journalists to students and engaged citizens.
about.dataP1: All data is sourced from the Stockholm International Peace Research Institute (SIPRI) Military Expenditure Database and the World Bank Open Data portal. Figures represent the most recently available annual data and are expressed in current US dollars.
about.contactP1: Have questions, feedback, or data correction requests? We'd love to hear from you.
